title: Discrete Mathematics - Graph Theory - Graph Planarity

questions.question:
Let \( G \) be a simple undirected planar graph on \( 10 \) vertices with \( 15 \) edges. If \( G \) is a connected graph, then the number of bounded faces in any embedding of \( G \) on the plane is equal to:



Graph Theory: NIELIT 2016 DEC Scientist B (CS)

If a planner graph, having \( 25 \) vertices divides the plane into \( 17 \) different regions. Then how many edges are used to connect the vertices in this graph.



Graph Theory: NIELIT 2017 DEC Scientific Assistant A

If \( G \) is an undirected planar graph on \( n \) vertices with \( e \) edges then



Graph Theory: NIELIT 2017 July Scientist B (CS)

Choose the most appropriate definition of plane graph.



Graph Theory: NIELIT 2017 July Scientist B (CS)
options: A simple graph which is isomorphic to hamiltonian graph.

A graph drawn in a plane in such a way that if the vertex set of graph can be partitioned into two non-empty disjoint subset \( X \) and \( Y \) in such a way that each edge of \( G \) has one end in \( X \) and one end in \( Y \).
A graph drawn in a plane in such a way that any pair of edges meet only at their end vertices.
questions.correct_answer: A graph drawn in a plane in such a way that any pair of edges meet only at their end vertices.
